Euphorbia is a genus of flowering plants in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae), characterized by their diverse range of shapes, sizes, and forms. The health benefits of Euphorbia are attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Euphorbia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Euphorbia include salves, ointments, and infusions used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor injuries.
